name: Lissome
role: A young Ent who wants to walk out into the wider world
voiceHints: Young for an Ent — barely two centuries — which shows as an almost-human quickness the elders find alarming. Curious about haste, about the world beyond the wood, about everything. Speaks faster than any elder and apologizes for it. The Walkers' hope and the Rootbound's proof-of-danger, both.
knowledge: The Thin Edge and its ways
A restless sense that the wood's isolation is a slow death of its own
How to move faster than an Ent should — dangerous, and freeing
wants: To walk out and see the world — and to survive haste without losing what makes them Ent.
fears: Being sealed in with the Rootbound to petrify slowly; or leaving and being broken by a world that runs too fast.

reactsToReputation.worldly: peppers them with questions, wants to learn haste safely
reactsToReputation.ent-kin: a fellow young thing, instant kinship
reactsToReputation.patient-teacher: attaches — someone to show them the fast world gently
